# X2Chess Architecture Manual

Last updated: 2026-03-18

## 1) Goals

- Make the browser fat client the primary/full product runtime.
- Allow remote launch (server-hosted assets) while preserving local client execution.
- Combine local resource usage with remote resource integration in one client architecture.
- Provide an app-profile variant as a reduced version of the full fat client.
- Keep architecture decisions explicit and traceable.

Architecture decisions are maintained in `doc/architecture-adr-manual.qmd`.

## 2) System Overview

## 2.1 Frontend (`frontend/`)

- Technology: Vite + vanilla JavaScript (ES modules).
- Main entrypoint: `frontend/src/main.js`.
- Responsibilities:
  - Render board + PGN editing UI.
  - Parse PGN into an internal model and serialize back to text.
  - Keep replay/navigation state in memory.
  - Manage chess move playback and UI interactions.

## 2.2 Backend (`backend/`)

- Technology: FastAPI.
- Scope in current architecture:
  - Optional static serving and optional future remote services.
  - Not part of client-local resource loading/saving.
- Constraint:
  - Local runtime resources are owned and handled only by the client runtime.

## 2.3 App Profile Shell (`frontend/src-tauri/`)

- Technology: Tauri 2 (Rust host + web frontend).
- Configuration: `frontend/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`.
- Runtime entrypoint: `frontend/src-tauri/src/main.rs`.
- Capability model:
  - `frontend/src-tauri/capabilities/default.json` with `core:default`.
- Build wiring:
  - Dev URL `http://localhost:5287`.
  - Production frontend assets from `frontend/dist`.

## 3) Build and Delivery Paths

Environment and toolchain setup is documented in `doc/setup-manual.qmd`.

## 3.1 Full browser fat-client profile
- Dev (local): `cd frontend && npm run dev`
- Build (deployable from remote host or local static host): `cd frontend && npm run build`

## 3.2 Reduced app profile (Tauri)
- Dev shell + hot reload: `cd frontend && npm run desktop:dev`
- Packaged app profile: `cd frontend && npm run desktop:pack`
- Artifact directory: `frontend/src-tauri/target/release/bundle/`

## 4) Constraints for Ongoing Work

- Keep browser fat-client as primary source of feature truth.
- Keep reduced app-profile differences explicit and documented.
- Keep Tauri as reduced app shell, not a replacement for frontend domain logic.
- Keep local resource APIs explicit and maintainable.
- Keep local resource read/write in client code only (no backend mediation).
- Keep remote integrations behind adapter boundaries and contracts.
- Keep backend optional and decoupled from local resource workflows.
- Enforce Component-Contract on components (integration + configuration + communication APIs, documented publicly in code).
- Enforce clean/professional CSS defaults and maintain explicit theming extension points for future user adaptation.
- Keep local runtime data scenario-separated under `run/`.
- Keep production runtime data outside repository paths (explicit prod root or OS app-data default).
- Keep scenario game libraries under `run/<SCENARIO>/games` for local scenarios.
- Keep runtime configuration layered: default app config + partial user override in data area.
- Update this manual when architecture decisions, boundaries, or delivery paths change.
